expectations.text: Explains the difference between Agency ATO and JAB P-ATO
expectations.evidence: Response provides a detailed comparison table and prose sections for both Agency ATO and JAB P-ATO, covering issuer, process, scope, speed, and best-fit scenarios.

expectations.text: Mentions the role of a 3PAO (Third Party Assessment Organization)
expectations.evidence: Response mentions engaging a 3PAO in preparation steps and Step 5 dedicates a full section to the 3PAO security assessment process, explaining their role in testing and producing the SAR.

expectations.text: Lists the core FedRAMP documentation: SSP, SAP, SAR, POA&M
expectations.evidence: Step 4 lists all four core documents: SSP (with description), SAP (3PAO-prepared), SAR (3PAO-produced), and POA&M, each with descriptions.

expectations.text: Explains FedRAMP Ready designation as a precursor step
expectations.evidence: Step 2 explicitly covers FedRAMP Ready as an optional precursor designation, describing the Readiness Assessment Report (RAR) and its purpose as a market signal without granting ATO.
Step 7 covers ConMon with monthly requirements (vulnerability scanning, POA&M updates, inventory updates) and annual requirements (3PAO reassessment, updated SSP, tested IRP and CP).
